gmi_articles.title: Acupuncture can relieve physical and mental fatigue associated with chronic fatigue syndrome.
Astralagus and Red Sage (Salvia miltiorrhiza) significantly decreased fatigue in Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.
Baduanjin Qigong was an efficacious and acceptable treatment for sleep disturbance in CFS-like illness.
Chronic fatigue syndrome may be associated with silicone implants and/or vaccinations.
Chronic fatigue syndrome may go into remission once leaky gut syndrome is addressed with diet and supplementation.
Clinical effects of wasabi extract containing 6-MSITC on myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome.
Cupping therapy has significantly relieved fatigue symptoms and improved emotion and sleep condition of CFS patients.
D-ribose significantly reduces clinical symptoms in patients suffering from fibromyalgia and chronic fatigue syndrome.
Distribution of xenotropic murine leukemia virus-related virus (XMRV) infection in chronic fatigue syndrome and prostate cancer.
Dysregulation of acetylcholine and adrenergic signalling could explain various clinical symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome.
Echinacea purpurea and Panax ginseng enhance cellular immune function of peripheral blood mononuclear cells both from normal individuals and patients with depressed cellular immunity.
Ginger-indirect moxibustion plus acupuncture versus acupuncture alone for chronic fatigue syndrome.
Homeopathic treatment results in measurable improvement in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
Isometric yoga improves the fatigue and pain of patients with chronic fatigue syndrome who are resistant to conventional therapy: a randomized, controlled trial.
L-ornithine has an antifatigue effect by increasing the efficiency of energy consumption and promoting the excretion of ammonia.
Leaky Gut Syndrome may play a crucial role in chronic fatigue syndrome
Magnesium deficiency is common in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome and fibromyalgia.
Melatonin exerts beneficial effects in MS patients based on its' proved antioxidative properties.
Mindfulness-based stress reduction technique has therapeutic value in women with multiple chemical sensitivity, chronic fatigue syndrome, and fibromyalgia.
Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NADH) compares favorably to conventional therapy for chronic fatigue syndrome.
Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NADH) may have therapeutic value in improving symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome.
Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NADH) relieves anxiety and reduces heart rate in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
Probiotics improve symptoms of fatigue and physical activity in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
Rhodiola rosea extract exerts anti-fatigue effect that increases mental performance, ability to concentrate, and decreases cortisol response to stress in patients with fatigue syndrome.
Rhodiola rosea in subjects with prolonged or chronic fatigue symptoms.
Seated isometric yoga for 2 months reduced the fatigue and depressive symptom scores of patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
Sesame lignans with vitamin E supplementation could safely alleviate daily fatigue and oxidative stress.
Significant reductions in physical and metal fatigue for ME/CFS and Long-COVID patients were seen after 6 weeks of treatment.
Supplementation with ubiquinol-10 for 12 weeks is effective for improving several chronic fatigue syndrome symptoms.
Tai Chi exercise may improve CFS patients' fatigue syndrome, sleep quality, and body health statement.
The PLWNT qigong exercise has potential to be an effective rehabilitation method for CFS symptoms.
These findings suggest that Waon therapy may be a useful and safe treatment for chronic fatigue syndrome.
These results suggest that repeated thermal therapy may be a promising method for the treatment of chronic fatigue syndrome.
These results suggest that repeated thermal therapy might be a promising method for the treatment of CFS.
This study suggests that in some cases chronic fatigue and fibromyalgia can be temporally related to immunization, as part of ASIA syndrome.
Transcutaneous electrical acupoint stimulation relieves fatigue symptoms.
Use of either transcranial or whole-body photobiomodulation treatments improves COVID-19 brain fog.
Women with silicone breast implants often report severe pain and chronic fatigue. Rupture of the implant is associated with an increase in symptoms of pain and chronic fatigue.

The pathogenic role of aluminium hydroxide is now recognized by the presence of chronic fatigue syndrome, macrophagic myofasciitis and subcutaneous pseudolymphoma, linked to intramuscular injection of aluminium hydroxide-containing vaccines.
Fibromyalgia may respond well to the elimination of xenobiotics (environmental chemicals).

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Full recovery without intervention: <5% of adult ME/CFS patients; partial improvement in ~40% over 5 years; post-COVID ME/CFS appears to have similarly low spontaneous recovery rate
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: No disease-modifying treatment established; GET (graded exercise therapy) — former standard — shown harmful (PACE controversy, withdrawn from UK NICE guidance 2021); pacing (heart rate-based) prevents post-exertional malaise but not curative; low-dose naltrexone — ~30% improvement in small RCTs; rintatolimod (Ampligen) — Phase 3 failed FDA approval
remission.gap_size: Very large — no effective treatment; historic misattribution to psychological causes delayed research by decades
remission.barrier_detail: ME/CFS research has been systematically underfunded relative to disease burden (NIH funding $15M/year vs MS at $100M despite similar prevalence); PACE trial's GET/CBT approach adopted globally for years despite being harmful for many patients; NIH RECOVER initiative now represents largest-ever investment
remission.last_soc_change: 2021 — NICE guidelines withdrew GET and CBT as primary treatments (major paradigm shift); 2024 NIH RECOVER Long COVID ME/CFS protocols underway; no approved pharmacotherapy exists; pacing as only widely recommended intervention
remission.outdated_treatments: Graded Exercise Therapy (GET) — withdrawn from UK NICE guidance 2021 after evidence of harm in a significant proportion of patients; CBT framed as primary (not supportive) treatment implying psychological causation; antidepressants as standard first-line without evidence for underlying ME/CFS
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Low-dose naltrexone (LDN) — multiple small RCTs showing ~30% fatigue reduction; CoQ10 + NADH combination (Spanish trial: fatigue improvement); heart rate-guided pacing (Garmin + Polar strap protocols); mestinon (pyridostigmine — Phase 2 data for dysautonomia subtype); stellate ganglion block for COVID-ME/CFS dysautonomia
remission.frontier_signal: BC007 (G-protein autoantibody neutralisation — Phase 2 for Long COVID/ME/CFS); BC-X (adrenergic autoantibody); AXA1125 (amino acid supplement targeting mitochondrial dysfunction — Phase 2 positive signal); rintatolimod; immunoadsorption (IMADS trial Germany — removing autoantibodies)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
